module

public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Abelian
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.Colimits
public import Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Sheaf.PullbackContinuous
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Presheaf
public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.OpenImmersion
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Bicategory.Adjunction.Adj
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Bicategory.Adjunction.Cat
public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Bicategory.Functor.LocallyDiscrete

/-!
# The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme

In this file, we define the abelian category of sheaves of modules
`X.Modules` over a scheme `X`, and study its basic functoriality.

-/

@[expose] public section

universe t u

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace SheafOfModules Bicategory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

variable {X Y Z T : Scheme.{u}}

variable (X) in
/-- The category of sheaves of modules over a scheme. -/
def Modules := SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf

namespace Modules

/-- Morphisms between `𝒪ₓ`-modules. Use `Hom.app` to act on sections. -/
def Hom (M N : X.Modules) : Type u := SheafOfModules.Hom M N

instance : Category X.Modules where
  Hom := Modules.Hom
  __ := (inferInstance : Category (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf))

noncomputable instance : Abelian X.Modules :=
  inferInstanceAs <| Abelian (SheafOfModules.{u} X.ringCatSheaf)
instance : HasLimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasLimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))
instance : HasColimits X.Modules := inferInstanceAs (HasColimits (SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf))

section Functor

variable (X) in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _

/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _

instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint

variable (X) in
/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _

instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=
  reflectsIsomorphisms_comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)

end Functor

variable {M N K : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} {U V : X.Opens}

section Presheaf

/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf

/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/
scoped[AlgebraicGeometry] notation3 "Γ(" M ", " U ")" => (Scheme.Modules.presheaf M).obj (.op U)

instance : Module Γ(X, U) Γ(M, U) := (M.val.obj (.op U)).isModule

variable (M) in
@[simp] lemma map_smul (i : U ⟶ V) (r : Γ(X, V)) (x : Γ(M, V)) :
    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=
  M.val.map_smul _ _ _

/-- The underlying map between abelian presheaves of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
noncomputable def Hom.mapPresheaf (φ : M ⟶ N) : M.presheaf ⟶ N.presheaf :=
  (toPresheaf X).map φ

/-- The application of a morphism of `𝒪ₓ`-modules to sections. -/
def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=
  (forget₂ _ _).map (φ.val.app (.op U))

@[simp] lemma mapPresheaf_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U) : φ.mapPresheaf.app U = φ.app U.unop := rfl

@[simp]
lemma Hom.app_smul (φ : M ⟶ N) (r : Γ(X, U)) (x : Γ(M, U)) :
    φ.app U (r • x) = r • φ.app U x :=
  (φ.val.app (.op U)).hom.map_smul r x

@[simp] lemma Hom.add_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ + ψ).app U = φ.app U + ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl

@[ext]
lemma hom_ext (f g : M ⟶ N) (H : ∀ U, f.app U = g.app U) : f = g := by
  apply SheafOfModules.hom_ext
  ext U x
  exact congr($(H U.unop) x)

lemma isSheaf (M : X.Modules) : M.presheaf.IsSheaf := SheafOfModules.isSheaf M

@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_obj : (toPresheaf X).obj M = M.presheaf := rfl
@[simp] lemma toPresheaf_map : (toPresheaf X).map φ = φ.mapPresheaf := rfl

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
lemma Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app {M N : X.Modules} {φ : M ⟶ N} :
    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
  rw [← isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso _ (toPresheaf X), NatTrans.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
  simp [Opposite.op_surjective.forall]

instance [IsIso φ] : IsIso (φ.app U) := Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mp ‹_› _

@[simp, push ←]
lemma inv_app [IsIso φ] : (inv φ).app U = inv (φ.app U) := by
  apply IsIso.eq_inv_of_hom_inv_id
  simp [← Hom.comp_app]

end Presheaf

noncomputable section Functorial

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

section

attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

end

variable (X) in
/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

variable (X) in
/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _

variable (X) in
lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
def pushforwardComp :
    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
def pullbackComp :
    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _

/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)

lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
    eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_left_unitality :
    (pullbackComp f (𝟙 Y)).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackId Y).hom (pullback f) ≫ (Functor.leftUnitor _).hom =
        eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (𝟙 _)
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId Y) (pullback f)
  let e₃ := (pullback f).leftUnitor
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_id_comp.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [← this]

@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_right_unitality :
    (pullbackComp (𝟙 X) f).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId X).hom ≫ (Functor.rightUnitor _).hom =
        eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp (𝟙 _) f
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_comp_id.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)

/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
these categories.) -/
@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
def pseudofunctor :
    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

end Functorial

noncomputable section Restriction

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]

/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
but has better defeqs. -/
def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
  · ext U x
    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
    exact congr($this x)

instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)

instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl

/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)

/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along open immersions commutes with taking stalks. -/
def restrictStalkNatIso (x : X) :
    restrictFunctor f ⋙ toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x ≅
    toPresheaf _ ⋙ TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ (f x) :=
  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
  (toPresheaf _ ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft (OpenNhds (f x))ᵒᵖ Y.Opensᵒᵖ Ab).obj
      (OpenNhds.inclusion (f x)).op).isoWhiskerLeft
      (Functor.Final.colimIso (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op)

@[simp]
lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
    ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU ≫
      (restrictStalkNatIso f x).hom.app M = M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) :=
  haveI := Functor.initial_of_adjunction (f.isOpenEmbedding.adjunctionNhds x)
  Functor.Final.ι_colimitIso_hom
    (f.isOpenEmbedding.functorNhds x).op
    ((OpenNhds.inclusion ((ConcreteCategory.hom f.base) x)).op ⋙ M.presheaf) _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[simp]
lemma germ_restrictStalkNatIso_inv_app (x : X) (M : Y.Modules) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
    M.presheaf.germ _ _ (by simpa) ≫ (restrictStalkNatIso f x).inv.app M =
      ((restrictFunctor f).obj M).presheaf.germ U _ hxU := by
  rw [← germ_restrictStalkNatIso_hom_app f x M hxU, Category.assoc, ← NatTrans.comp_app,
    Iso.hom_inv_id]
  simp

end Restriction

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — restriction of modules to a basic open -/

namespace Modules

variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (f : R)

/-- The basic open `D(f) ⊆ Spec R`, as an open subscheme of `Spec R`. -/
abbrev specBasicOpen : (Spec R).Opens := PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen f

/-- The localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R` factoring through `D(f)`: the inverse of the
affine identification `D(f) ≅ Spec R_f` followed by the open immersion of `D(f)`. -/
noncomputable abbrev specAwayToSpec :
    Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away f)) ⟶ Spec R :=
  (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv ≫ (specBasicOpen f).ι

/-- **Stacks 01I8, `lemma-widetilde-pullback`.** Restriction of an `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module to the basic
open `D(f)`, transported to a sheaf of `𝒪_{Spec R_f}`-modules along the affine identification
`D(f) ≅ Spec R_f`.  Built by restricting first along the open immersion `D(f) ↪ Spec R`, then along
the (iso, hence open) inverse of `basicOpenIsoSpecAway f`.  Project-local: assembles two Mathlib
restrictions into the single transport used by the affine quasi-coherence equivalence. -/
noncomputable def modulesRestrictBasicOpen (F : (Spec R).Modules) :
    (Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away f))).Modules :=
  (F.restrict (specBasicOpen f).ι).restrict (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv

/-- **Stacks 01I8, `lemma-widetilde-pullback`.** The transported restriction
`modulesRestrictBasicOpen f F` is canonically isomorphic to the inverse image of `F` along the
localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R`.  This identifies the double restriction with a single
pullback, and is the comparison isomorphism `F|_{D(f)} ≅ F_{(f)}` of the blueprint.  Project-local:
reconciles the (good-defeq) iterated `restrict` with the conceptual inverse-image description. -/
noncomputable def modulesRestrictBasicOpenIso (F : (Spec R).Modules) :
    modulesRestrictBasicOpen f F ≅
      (Scheme.Modules.pullback (specAwayToSpec f)).obj F :=
  ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorComp (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv (specBasicOpen f).ι).app F).symm
    ≪≫ (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback (specAwayToSpec f)).app F

end Modules

/-- The localisation morphism `Spec R_f ⟶ Spec R` of `specAwayToSpec` is exactly the spectrum of the
away-localisation ring map `R → R_f`.  Project-local: identifies the geometric transport map with
the algebraic `Spec.map (algebraMap …)`, so that pullback along it computes base change. -/
theorem specAwayToSpec_eq :
    specAwayToSpec f = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away f))) := by
  rw [specAwayToSpec, Iso.inv_comp_eq]
  exact (IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq_hom_fac _ _ _).symm

end AlgebraicGeometry

## Summary

  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`, `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde`,

  - `AlgebraicGeometry.specBasicOpen` (helper abbrev, line 25)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.specAwayToSpec` (helper abbrev, line 29)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.modulesRestrictBasicOpen` (**L1 named target**, line 38)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.modulesRestrictBasicOpenIso` (**L1 named target**, line 47)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.specAwayToSpec_eq` (L2 feeder, line 55)
- **Declarations blocked (2):** `tilde_restrict_basicOpen` (L2),
  `presentation_restrict_basicOpen` (L3) — NOT added (no sorry left). Both blocked on the genuine
  Stacks 01I8 *base-change compatibility of `tilde`*, which is absent from Mathlib (see below).
- **sorry count:** 0 → 0 (new file, never had a sorry).

## L1 — RESOLVED (both named targets, axiom-clean)
- **`modulesRestrictBasicOpen f F`** : `(Spec R).Modules → (Spec R_f).Modules`,
  `R_f = Localization.Away f`. Built as the iterated Mathlib restriction
  `(F.restrict (specBasicOpen f).ι).restrict (basicOpenIsoSpecAway f).inv`:
  first restrict along the open immersion `D(f) ↪ Spec R` (`Scheme.Opens.ι`), then along the
  inverse of the affine identification `basicOpenIsoSpecAway f : D(f) ≅ Spec R_f` (an iso, hence an
  open immersion — instance synthesised automatically). Object form (takes `F`); functoriality is
  carried by the underlying `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor`s.
- **`modulesRestrictBasicOpenIso f F`** : `modulesRestrictBasicOpen f F ≅ (pullback (specAwayToSpec f)).obj F`.
  Built from `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorComp` (double-restrict = restrict-along-composite) and
  `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback` (restrict ≅ pullback). This is the blueprint comparison
  iso `F|_{D(f)} ≅ F_{(f)}`, stated in the conceptually-correct *inverse-image-along-localisation*
  form. **Approach:** direct composition of the two Mathlib natural isos `.app F`. RESOLVED.

## specAwayToSpec_eq — RESOLVED (axiom-clean, L2 feeder)
- `specAwayToSpec f = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R (Localization.Away f)))`.
- **Approach:** `rw [specAwayToSpec, Iso.inv_comp_eq]` then `(IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq_hom_fac _ _ _).symm`,
  exploiting that `basicOpenIsoSpecAway` is *definitionally* `IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq (Opens.ι _)
  (Spec.map (ofHom (algebraMap …)))`. RESOLVED.
- **Why it matters:** combined with `modulesRestrictBasicOpenIso`, it rewrites L1's transport as the
  honest pullback along `Spec.map (algebraMap R R_f)`. So L2 reduces to: *pullback of `tilde M` along
  `Spec.map (algebraMap)` is `tilde` of the base-changed module* — the single remaining gap.

## tilde_restrict_basicOpen (L2) — NOT ADDED (genuine Mathlib gap)
- **Reduced statement (after L1 + `specAwayToSpec_eq`):**
  `(Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap R R_f)))).obj (tilde M)
    ≅ tilde (ModuleCat.of R_f (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M))`.
  i.e. **`tilde` commutes with pullback/base-change along `Spec.map` of a ring map.**
- **Approach 1 (search for the compatibility lemma):** `lean_loogle "AlgebraicGeometry.tilde,
  Scheme.Modules.pullback"` → *no results*. `lean_leansearch` for "pullback of tilde = tilde of base
  change" and "inverse image of associated sheaf on basic open" → only returns `SheafOfModules.pullback`,
  `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `ModuleCat.Tilde.openToLocalization`, `stalkIso`. **No base-change /
  pullback compatibility of `tilde` exists in Mathlib.** This is exactly Stacks 01I8
  `lemma-widetilde-pullback`, which Mathlib has not formalised.
- **Approach 2 (section-level localisation):** Mathlib *does* give the section fact

  **Planner: bundle these three under the `lem:modules_restrict_basicOpen` `\lean{…}` list (or give

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.

- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.

4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?

22	* `tilde_preservesFiniteColimits` — the *right*-exactness half: `~` is a left adjoint
23	  (`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction`), hence preserves all colimits, in particular finite
24	  colimits.  This is one of the two halves of exactness.
25	* `tilde_toStalk_map_injective` — the *flatness core* in the only publicly-accessible form:
26	  for an injective `R`-module map `f : M ⟶ N`, the localised stalk map
27	  `M_𝔭 →ₗ[R] N_𝔭` (built from Mathlib's `IsLocalizedModule (tilde.toStalk · x)` instances) is
28	  injective.  This is the algebraic content that "localisation `R → R_𝔭` is flat" contributes
29	  to mono-preservation.
30	
31	## What is delivered (axiom-clean), continued
32	
33	* `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk` — the **germ-naturality** transport identity (the crux flagged
34	  by the planner).  For `f : M ⟶ N` and a point `x ↔ 𝔭`, the `Ab`-valued stalk map of `~f`
35	  (computed through the faithful, limit-preserving `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf`) intertwines the two
36	  localisation maps `tilde.toStalk`: `toStalk M x ≫ (Ab-stalk map) = f ≫ toStalk N x`.  This
37	  identifies the otherwise opaque `Ab`-germ-induced stalk map with the localised module map of `f`
38	  on the image of `toStalk`.  Proven on the public `Ab`-stalk path (`stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply` +
39	  the `⊤`-section naturality `StructureSheaf.comapₗ_const`), avoiding the module-private handles.
40	* `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` — the **categorical reduction**: since
41	  `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` reflects finite limits (faithful + preserves limits + reflects isos,
42	  and `(Spec R).Modules` has finite limits), `~` preserves finite limits as soon as the composite
43	  `~ ⋙ toPresheaf` does.  This is `Limits.preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves`.
44	
45	## Why `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` itself is NOT closed here
46	
47	The mathematical route (blueprint `lem:tilde_preserves_kernels`) is stalkwise flatness.  The
48	**categorical-glue obstruction is FALSE** — `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` (above)
49	discharges it via `preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves`; no "right-exact + mono ⟹
50	left-exact" lemma is needed.  The single remaining gap is the **stalkwise localisation step**:
51	showing the composite `~ ⋙ Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` preserves finite limits.  Stalkwise this is
52	exactly localisation-is-flat:
53	* The `Ab`-germ-induced stalk map of `~f` is identified on the image of `toStalk` with the localised
54	  map of `f` by `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk`; and the localised map is injective
55	  (`tilde_toStalk_map_injective`).
56	* What still has to be built (~100–150 LOC): upgrade this to a full stalkwise *isomorphism* of the
57	  kernel-comparison map.  Concretely, (a) prove the `Ab` stalk map is `R`-linear (via `germₗ` +
58	  `R`-linearity of `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app`) so it equals `IsLocalizedModule.map …`, then (b)
59	  feed `∀ x, PreservesFiniteLimits (~ ⋙ toPresheaf ⋙ stalkFunctor x)` through a jointly-reflecting
60	  stalk family (`CategoryTheory.JointlyReflectIsomorphisms.jointlyReflectsLimit`) to obtain
61	  `PreservesFiniteLimits (~ ⋙ toPresheaf)`, then `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf`.
62	  The `ModuleCat R`-valued stalk path is dead (Mathlib privacy of `toStalkₗ'`, `stalkIsoₗ`,
63	  `stalkToLocalizationₗ`, `structurePresheafInModuleCat`); use the public `Ab` path throughout.

74	variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
75	
76	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — exactness of the tilde functor -/
77	
78	/-- **Right-exactness of `~`.**  The tilde functor `~ : ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules`
79	preserves finite colimits: it is a left adjoint (`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.adjunction`), so it
80	preserves all colimits.  Project-local because the packaged statement is what the
81	kernel/cokernel quasi-coherence argument (Stacks `lemma-kernel-cokernel-quasi-coherent`)
82	consumes alongside the (still open) finite-limit half. -/
83	theorem tilde_preservesFiniteColimits :
84	    Limits.PreservesFiniteColimits (tilde.functor R) := inferInstance
85	
86	/-- **Flatness core of mono-preservation for `~`.**  For an injective `R`-module map
87	`f : M ⟶ N` and a point `x ↔ 𝔭` of `Spec R`, the induced localised map on stalks
88	`M_𝔭 →ₗ[R] N_𝔭` — assembled from Mathlib's `IsLocalizedModule (tilde.toStalk · x).hom`
89	instances via `IsLocalizedModule.map` — is injective.  This is exactly the contribution of
90	"localisation `R → R_𝔭` is flat" to the statement that `~` preserves monomorphisms (Stacks
91	01HV, exactness of `~`).  Stated with the publicly-accessible stalk-localisation map
92	`AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk`, the only such handle exported by Mathlib. -/
93	theorem tilde_toStalk_map_injective {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N)
94	    (hf : Function.Injective f.hom) (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
95	    Function.Injective (IsLocalizedModule.map x.asIdeal.primeCompl
96	      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk M x).hom (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk N x).hom f.hom) :=
97	  IsLocalizedModule.map_injective _ _ _ _ hf
98	
99	/-- **Reduction of the named target.**  `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` follows once `~` is shown to
100	preserve every kernel `parallelPair f 0`; all the ambient typeclass hypotheses of
101	`Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels` are already discharged for
102	`tilde.functor R` (it is additive, `ModuleCat R` and `(Spec R).Modules` have the requisite
103	finite (co)products / zero objects).  Recorded project-locally so the remaining obligation is a
104	single, sharply-stated hypothesis for the continuation lane. -/
105	theorem tilde_preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels
106	    (H : ∀ {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N),
107	      PreservesLimit (parallelPair f 0) (tilde.functor R)) :
108	    PreservesFiniteLimits (tilde.functor R) :=
109	  Functor.preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels _
110	
111	/-- **Germ-naturality of the localisation map `toStalk`.**  For an `R`-module map
112	`f : M ⟶ N` and a point `x ↔ 𝔭` of `Spec R`, the `Ab`-valued stalk map of `~f` (computed via the
113	faithful, limit-preserving forgetful functor `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf`) intertwines the two
114	localisation maps `tilde.toStalk`: precomposing with `toStalk M x` and postcomposing `f` agree.
115	
116	This is the load-bearing transport identity for mono/kernel preservation of `~`: it identifies the
117	otherwise opaque `Ab`-germ-induced stalk map with the localised module map of `f`.  Project-local
118	because Mathlib only exports the localisation handles `tilde.toStalk` and `toOpenₗ` and the section
119	naturality `comapₗ_const`; the germ-level statement is assembled here. -/
120	theorem tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N)
121	    (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) (m : M) :
122	    (TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x).map
123	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (tilde.map f))
124	        ((tilde.toStalk M x).hom m)
125	      = (tilde.toStalk N x).hom (f.hom m) := by
126	  change (TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x).map
127	        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (tilde.map f))
128	        (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (AlgebraicGeometry.moduleStructurePresheaf R M).presheaf ⊤ x
129	          (by trivial) (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R M ⊤ m))
130	      = TopCat.Presheaf.germ (AlgebraicGeometry.moduleStructurePresheaf R N).presheaf ⊤ x
131	          (by trivial) (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R N ⊤ (f.hom m))
132	  erw [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply ⊤ x True.intro
133	    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (tilde.map f)) (StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ R M ⊤ m)]
134	  congr 1
135	  simp only [Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf_map, Scheme.Modules.mapPresheaf_app,
136	    Scheme.Modules.Hom.app]
137	  rw [StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ_eq_const, StructureSheaf.toOpenₗ_eq_const]
138	  simp only [AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map, AlgebraicGeometry.SpecModulesToSheafFullyFaithful,
139	    CategoryTheory.NatTrans.comp_app, AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.modulesSpecToSheafIso,
140	    ModuleCat.hom_comp]
141	  erw [StructureSheaf.comapₗ_const (hb := le_of_eq PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one.symm)]
142	  rfl
143	
144	/-- **Reduction of `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` to the presheaf level.**  The forgetful functor
145	`Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` from `𝒪_{Spec R}`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups is faithful,
146	preserves limits, and reflects isomorphisms; hence (since `(Spec R).Modules` has finite limits) it
147	reflects finite limits.  Therefore, to show `~` preserves finite limits it suffices to show the
148	composite `~ ⋙ toPresheaf` does.  This isolates the remaining obligation of
149	`lem:tilde_preserves_kernels` to a statement about the abelian-presheaf-valued composite, whose
150	stalks are computed by `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk`.  Project-local categorical glue (it refutes
151	the earlier-feared "no right-exact + mono ⟹ left-exact" obstruction: the reduction is purely
152	`preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves`). -/
153	theorem tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf
154	    (H : PreservesFiniteLimits
155	      (tilde.functor R ⋙ Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R)))) :
156	    PreservesFiniteLimits (tilde.functor R) :=
157	  haveI := H
158	  Limits.preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves (tilde.functor R)
159	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R)))
160	
161	end AlgebraicGeometry

4329	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits, AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_preservesFiniteColimits, AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_toStalk_map_injective, AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_preservesFiniteLimits_of_preservesKernels, AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk, AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf}
4330	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, \texttt{lemma-kernel-cokernel-quasi-coherent}
4331	  % (proof) (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1425--1432).
4332	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "This follows from the exactness of the functor $\widetilde{\ }$
4333	  % since by Lemma \ref{lemma-compare-constructions} we know that any map
4334	  % $\psi : \widetilde{M} \to \widetilde{N}$ comes from
4335	  % an $R$-module map $\varphi : M \to N$. (So we have
4336	  % $\Ker(\psi) = \widetilde{\Ker(\varphi)}$ and
4337	  % $\Coker(\psi) = \widetilde{\Coker(\varphi)}$.)"
4338	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes,
4339	  \texttt{lemma-kernel-cokernel-quasi-coherent} (and Tag 01HV, exactness of
4340	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\)).}
4341	  Let \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\). The functor \(M \mapsto \widetilde{M}\) from
4342	  \(R\)-modules to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is exact; in particular it preserves
4343	  kernels: for an \(R\)-module map \(\varphi : M \to N\) the induced
4344	  \(\widetilde{\varphi} : \widetilde{M} \to \widetilde{N}\) has
4345	  \(\operatorname{Ker}(\widetilde{\varphi}) = \widetilde{\operatorname{Ker}(\varphi)}\),
4346	  and more generally \(\widetilde{(-)}\) carries finite limits of \(R\)-modules to the
4347	  corresponding limits of associated sheaves. The tilde functor is already known to be
4348	  additive and to preserve colimits; its preservation of finite limits (kernels) is the
4349	  property needed here and is supplied as a dedicated lemma.
4350	\end{lemma}
4351	
4352	% NOTE: The Lean instance AlgebraicGeometry.tildePreservesFiniteLimits
4353	% (PreservesFiniteLimits / PreservesKernels for the tilde functor
4354	% ~ : ModuleCat R ⥤ (Spec R).Modules) is ABSENT from Mathlib and is
4355	% project-to-build (Route-P P3 sub-gap). Mathlib provides the tilde functor
4356	% and its stalk computation but not the packaged finite-limit-preservation
4357	% instance; the informal proof below is the stalkwise-flatness argument the
4358	% eventual prover formalizes.
4359	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Let us compute the stalk of $\widetilde M$ at a point
4360	% $x \in \Spec(R)$. Suppose that $x$ corresponds to the prime
4361	% $\mathfrak p \subset R$. By definition of the stalk we see that
4362	% $\widetilde M_x = \colim_{f\in R, f\not\in \mathfrak p} M_f$ ...
4363	% Hence by Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-localization-colimit}
4364	% we conclude that $\widetilde M_x = M_{\mathfrak p}$."
4365	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The exactness of the functor $M \mapsto \widetilde M$
4366	% follows from the fact that the functor $M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak p}$
4367	% is exact and the fact that exactness of short exact sequences
4368	% may be checked on stalks, see Modules, Lemma \ref{modules-lemma-abelian}."
4369	\begin{proof}
4370	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (item~(7) and
4371	  proof), Tag 01HV.}
4372	  Kernels in the category of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules are computed
4373	  objectwise and sheafified, and isomorphisms of sheaves may be checked on stalks: a
4374	  morphism of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules is an isomorphism if and only if it induces an
4375	  isomorphism on every stalk. So it suffices to compare stalks. By the stalk computation
4376	  for the tilde functor (\texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} item~(7)) the stalk of
4377	  \(\widetilde{M}\) at a point \(x \in \operatorname{Spec} R\) corresponding to the prime
4378	  \(\mathfrak{p}\) is the localisation
4379	  \(\widetilde{M}_x = M_{\mathfrak{p}}\), naturally in \(M\): the stalk functor at \(x\)
4380	  applied to \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is the localisation functor \(M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak{p}}\).
4381	
4382	  Now let \(\varphi : M \to N\) be an \(R\)-module map with kernel
4383	  \(K = \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi)\), giving the exact sequence
4384	  \(0 \to K \to M \xrightarrow{\varphi} N\). Localisation \(R \to R_{\mathfrak{p}}\) is
4385	  flat, hence the functor \(M \mapsto M_{\mathfrak{p}}\) is exact and in particular
4386	  preserves kernels: \(K_{\mathfrak{p}} = \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi_{\mathfrak{p}} :
4387	  M_{\mathfrak{p}} \to N_{\mathfrak{p}})\). Translating through the stalk identification,
4388	  the natural comparison map
4389	  \(\widetilde{K} \to \operatorname{Ker}(\widetilde{\varphi})\) induces, at every point
4390	  \(x \leftrightarrow \mathfrak{p}\), the isomorphism \(K_{\mathfrak{p}} \xrightarrow{\sim}
4391	  \operatorname{Ker}(\varphi_{\mathfrak{p}})\) (using that the stalk functor, being a
4392	  filtered colimit, is itself exact and commutes with forming the kernel). Being a
4393	  stalkwise isomorphism, the comparison map is an isomorphism of sheaves, so
4394	  \(\operatorname{Ker}(\widetilde{\varphi}) = \widetilde{\operatorname{Ker}(\varphi)}\).
4395	  The same stalkwise-flatness argument applied to a finite diagram of \(R\)-modules shows
4396	  that \(\widetilde{(-)}\) carries finite limits to finite limits; equivalently
4397	  \(\widetilde{(-)}\) is left exact, i.e.\ preserves finite limits (kernels).
4398	
4399	  \emph{Mechanization remarks (the three sub-steps the formalization rests on).}
4400	  \begin{itemize}
4401	    \item \textbf{(A) The stalk comparison is \(R_{\mathfrak{p}}\)-linear, not merely
4402	      additive.} The stalk of \(\widetilde{M}\) at \(x\) is computed as a stalk of the
4403	      underlying sheaf of abelian groups, so the induced stalk map is \emph{a priori}
4404	      only a map of abelian groups. To know it is the localised \(R\)-module map
4405	      \(M_{\mathfrak{p}} \to N_{\mathfrak{p}}\) — and hence injective/exact by the
4406	      flatness of localisation — one identifies it on germs with the linear localisation
4407	      map: the germ \(\operatorname{germ}_x\) is \(R_{\mathfrak{p}}\)-linear (the linear
4408	      germ \(\operatorname{germ}_\ell\)), and the stalk action of the abelian-group stalk
4409	      functor on \(\widetilde{\varphi}\) agrees with \(\varphi\) on the image of the
4410	      structure map \(\widetilde{M} \to \widetilde{M}_x\). This germ-naturality identity
4411	      is the load-bearing step.
4412	      % NOTE: realized by the helper `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk` (the germ-naturality
4413	      % transport, axiom-clean iter-034) together with the public linear germ
4414	      % `StructureSheaf.germₗ` and the Γ-linearity of `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app`; this packages
4415	      % the abelian-group stalk map `σ_x` as the R-linear localisation map and identifies it
4416	      % with `IsLocalizedModule.map …`, whose injectivity is the already-built
4417	      % `tilde_toStalk_map_injective`.
4418	    \item \textbf{(B) Stalkwise isomorphism \(\Rightarrow\) isomorphism of sheaves.} A
4419	      morphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules that is an isomorphism on every
4420	      stalk is an isomorphism; equivalently the family of stalk functors jointly reflects
4421	      isomorphisms (and jointly reflects the finite limits being compared). One checks the
4422	      kernel-comparison map is a stalk-wise isomorphism by (A), then concludes it is an
4423	      isomorphism of sheaves of modules.
4424	      % NOTE: in Lean, reduce to the underlying abelian-presheaf comparison and use the
4425	      % jointly-reflecting stalk family on a sheaf (e.g. `JointlyReflectIsomorphisms` /
4426	      % `Sheaf.forget ⋙ stalkFunctor x` preserving finite limits), via
4427	      % `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` (faithful, `ReflectsIsomorphisms`, `PreservesLimits`);
4428	      % the ModuleCat-valued stalk path is DEAD (Mathlib privacy of `toStalkₗ'`/`stalkIsoₗ`).
4429	    \item \textbf{(C) Reduction to the underlying abelian-presheaf composite.} The
4430	      finite-limit-preservation question for \(\widetilde{(-)}\) into sheaves of modules is
4431	      equivalent to the same question for the composite of \(\widetilde{(-)}\) with the
4432	      forgetful functor to the underlying presheaf of abelian groups, where the
4433	      sections-level localisation flatness from (A)–(B) applies directly. This reduction
4434	      lets the proof work entirely at the abelian-presheaf/stalk level.
4435	      % NOTE: realized by the helper `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` (axiom-clean
4436	      % iter-034) via `preservesFiniteLimits_of_reflects_of_preserves`: it reduces
4437	      % `PreservesFiniteLimits (~)` to `PreservesFiniteLimits (~ ⋙ Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf)`,
4438	      % and the named target assembles from that composite once (A)+(B) are in place.
4439	  \end{itemize}
4440	\end{proof}
4441	
4442	\begin{lemma}[The kernel of a generating epimorphism is quasi-coherent]
4443	  \label{lem:qcoh_kernel_qcoh}
4444	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.qcoh_kernel_qcoh}
4445	  \uses{lem:qcoh_global_generation, lem:tilde_preserves_kernels, lem:free_isQuasicoherent}
4446	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Schemes, \texttt{lemma-kernel-cokernel-quasi-coherent}
4447	  % (read from references/stacks-schemes.tex, L1418--1432).

    True := by
  let σ := (TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x).map
    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map f))

  have : σ = σ := rfl

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — R-linear packaging of the Ab-stalk map -/

/-- **Sub-step (A): the `Ab`-stalk map `σ_x` is `R`-linear.**  The stalk-functor image
`σ_x := (stalkFunctor Ab x).map (toPresheaf.map (~f))` is a priori only an `Ab`-morphism between
the stalks; this packages it as the genuine `R`-linear map `M_𝔭 →ₗ[R] N_𝔭`.  Project-local: the
`ModuleCat R`-valued stalk functor for `SheafOfModules` is not exported (its building blocks
`stalkIsoₗ`/`toStalkₗ'` are private), so the linear structure is reconstructed here on the public
`Ab` stalk via germ-linearity. -/
noncomputable def stalkMapₗ {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M).presheaf.stalk x →ₗ[R]
      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde N).presheaf.stalk x where
  toFun := (TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x).map
    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map f))
  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
  map_smul' r ζ := by
    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
    sorry

end AlgebraicGeometry

    (hxU : x ∈ U) (r : R) (s : Γ(AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M, U)) :

import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry TopCat.Presheaf TopologicalSpace
universe u
variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}}

-- Is the R-scalar on the stalk literally toStalk-action (compHom rfl)?
example {M : ModuleCat R} (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) (r : R)
    (ζ : (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M).presheaf.stalk x) :
    r • ζ = (StructureSheaf.toStalk R x).hom r • ζ := rfl

-- key conversion: r • germ_M s = germ_M (algebraMap r • s)
example {M : ModuleCat R} (U : Opens (PrimeSpectrum.Top R)) (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R)
    (hxU : x ∈ U) (r : R) (s : (AlgebraicGeometry.moduleStructurePresheaf R M).obj (.op U)) :
    r • (ConcreteCategory.hom
          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (AlgebraicGeometry.moduleStructurePresheaf R M).presheaf U x hxU)) s
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom
          (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (AlgebraicGeometry.moduleStructurePresheaf R M).presheaf U x hxU))
          ((algebraMap R ((structureSheafInType R R).obj.obj (.op U)) r) • s) := by
  rw [PresheafOfModules.germ_smul, StructureSheaf.algebraMap_germ_apply]

instance (U : (Opens (PrimeSpectrum.Top R))ᵒᵖ) :
    Algebra R ((structureSheafInType R A).obj.obj U) :=
  (sectionsSubalgebra A U.unop).algebra

local notation "Γ(" M ", " U ")" =>
  (Functor.obj (ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.obj (structureSheafInType _ M))) (Opposite.op U)

@[simp]
lemma structureSheafInType.add_apply {U : Opens (PrimeSpectrum.Top R)} (s t : Γ(M, U)) (x : U) :
  (s + t).1 x = s.1 x + t.1 x := rfl


end Public

local notation "Γ(" M ", " U ")" =>
  (Functor.obj (ObjectProperty.FullSubcategory.obj (structureSheafInType _ M))) (Opposite.op U)

namespace StructureSheaf

section basicOpen

    (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) (hxU : x ∈ U) :
    ((structureSheafInType (R := R) R M).obj.obj (.op U)) →ₗ[R]

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — R-linear packaging of the Ab-stalk map -/

/-- **Sub-step (A): the `Ab`-stalk map `σ_x` is `R`-linear.**  The stalk-functor image
`σ_x := (stalkFunctor Ab x).map (toPresheaf.map (~f))` is a priori only an `Ab`-morphism between
the stalks; this packages it as the genuine `R`-linear map `M_𝔭 →ₗ[R] N_𝔭`.  Project-local: the
`ModuleCat R`-valued stalk functor for `SheafOfModules` is not exported (its building blocks
`stalkIsoₗ`/`toStalkₗ'` are private), so the linear structure is reconstructed here on the public
`Ab` stalk via germ-linearity. -/
noncomputable def stalkMapₗ {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M).presheaf.stalk x →ₗ[R]
      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde N).presheaf.stalk x where
  toFun := (TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x).map
    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map f))
  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
  map_smul' r ζ := by
    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
    obtain ⟨U, hxU, s, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M).presheaf x ζ
    sorry

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — R-linear packaging of the Ab-stalk map -/

/-- **Germ–scalar compatibility (`R`-linearity of the germ map).**  For `s` a section of `M^~`
over `U` and `r : R`, the germ at `x` of `(algebraMap R Γ(O_X,U) r) • s` equals `r •` the germ of
`s` (the `R`-action on the stalk being the localisation one through `tilde.toStalk R x`).  This is
the section-level half of "the `Ab`-stalk map of `~f` is `R`-linear".  Project-local because the
linear germ `StructureSheaf.germₗ` is not exported (no `public`); it is rebuilt here from the public
`PresheafOfModules.germ_smul` and `StructureSheaf.algebraMap_germ_apply`. -/
theorem tilde_germ_algebraMap_smul {M : ModuleCat R} (U : (Spec (.of R)).Opens)
    (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) (hxU : x ∈ U) (r : R) (s : Γ(AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M, U)) :
    (ConcreteCategory.hom
        (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (AlgebraicGeometry.moduleStructurePresheaf R M).presheaf U x hxU))
        ((algebraMap R Γ(Spec (.of R), U) r) • s)
      = r • (ConcreteCategory.hom
        (TopCat.Presheaf.germ (AlgebraicGeometry.moduleStructurePresheaf R M).presheaf U x hxU)) s := by
  erw [PresheafOfModules.germ_smul, StructureSheaf.algebraMap_germ_apply]
  rfl

/-- **Sub-step (A): the `Ab`-stalk map `σ_x` is `R`-linear.**  The stalk-functor image
`σ_x := (stalkFunctor Ab x).map (toPresheaf.map (~f))` is a priori only an `Ab`-morphism between
the stalks; this packages it as the genuine `R`-linear map `M_𝔭 →ₗ[R] N_𝔭`.  Project-local: the
`ModuleCat R`-valued stalk functor for `SheafOfModules` is not exported (its building blocks
`stalkIsoₗ`/`toStalkₗ'` are private), so the linear structure is reconstructed here on the public
`Ab` stalk via germ-linearity. -/
noncomputable def stalkMapₗ {M N : ModuleCat R} (f : M ⟶ N) (x : PrimeSpectrum.Top R) :
    (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M).presheaf.stalk x →ₗ[R]
      (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde N).presheaf.stalk x where
  toFun := (TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor _ x).map
    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map f))
  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
  map_smul' r ζ := by
    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
    obtain ⟨U, hxU, s, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde M).presheaf x ζ
    rw [← tilde_germ_algebraMap_smul U x hxU r s,
      TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply U x hxU
        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map f)),
      TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply U x hxU
        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf (Spec (.of R))).map (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.map f)),
      Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf_map, Scheme.Modules.mapPresheaf_app,
      Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul, tilde_germ_algebraMap_smul]

    (fun s => IsLocalizedModule.map_units (AlgebraicGeometry.tilde.toStalk N x).hom s)

  rw [IsLocalizedModule.map_apply]
  exact tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk f x m

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/TildeExactness.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added: 4, all axiom-clean** (`{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):
  - `tilde_germ_algebraMap_smul` (line 169) — germ–scalar compatibility (germ is `R`-linear via the
    public `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul` + `StructureSheaf.algebraMap_germ_apply`).
  - `stalkMapₗ` (line 185, `noncomputable def`) — **sub-step (A): `σ_x` packaged as an `R`-LINEAR map**
    `(tilde M).presheaf.stalk x →ₗ[R] (tilde N).presheaf.stalk x`. The `map_smul'` field is the new,
    previously-missing content.
  - `stalkMapₗ_eq` (line 210) — **the σ_x = localised-map identification**:
    `stalkMapₗ f x = IsLocalizedModule.map _ (toStalk M x).hom (toStalk N x).hom f.hom`.
  - `stalkMapₗ_injective` (line 226) — stalkwise injectivity of `~f` for a mono `f` (combines the two
    above with `tilde_toStalk_map_injective`).
- **Declarations blocked: 1** — the named target `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` (sub-steps (B)+(C),
  jointly-reflecting stalk assembly). NOT added (no sorry left). See "Why I stopped".
- **sorry count: 0 → 0** across the file (the iter began with the helper-only file; the named target
  was absent, not a sorry — it remains absent).

This is exactly the partial the iter-035 plan named as preferable to a typed pin: *"the R-linear σ_x
packaging + the σ_x=localised-map identification > a typed pin."* Both are now axiom-clean.

## tilde_germ_algebraMap_smul (line 169) — RESOLVED
- **Approach:** Re-derive the (unexported, non-`public`) `StructureSheaf.germₗ.map_smul` recipe.
  `erw [PresheafOfModules.germ_smul, StructureSheaf.algebraMap_germ_apply]; rfl`.
- **Key friction (documented for reuse):** must use `erw` (not `rw`) — the structure-sheaf ring
  section appears in ≥3 defeq-but-not-syntactic forms (`Γ(Spec (.of R), U)`,
  `(structurePresheafInCommRingCat R).obj (op U)`, `(structureSheafInType R R).obj.obj (op U)`); the
  scalar-action / algebra instances are registered on different ones. Stating the germ on
  `(tilde M).presheaf.germ` (not `(moduleStructurePresheaf R M).presheaf.germ`) is what lets the
  caller's `rw` match.

## stalkMapₗ (line 185) — RESOLVED (the crux of this iter)
- **Approach:** `LinearMap` whose `toFun` is the public `Ab`-stalk map
  `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map (toPresheaf.map (~f))`; `map_smul'` proved by `germ_exist` + the chain
  `r•germ s = germ(a•s)` (helper, reversed) → `stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply` → `Hom.app_smul`
  (`Scheme.Modules.Hom.app` is `O_X(U)`-linear) → helper (forward) → `rfl`.
- **Documented friction (do NOT rediscover):**
  - The `map_smul'` goal must be proved *inside the `LinearMap` structure*; stating `r • σ ζ` standalone
    fails `HSMul` synthesis (stalk codomain only DEFEQ to `(tilde N).presheaf.stalk x`). The plan's
    warning is correct; the structure field pins the types.
  - `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app (~f) U : Γ(~M,U) ⟶ Γ(~N,U)` lands in **`Ab`**, NOT `ModuleCat O_X(U)`.
    Its `O_X(U)`-linearity is the dedicated lemma **`Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul`** (found via `exact?`),
    not `map_smul` of a `.hom`.
  - After `mapPresheaf_app` the section index is `unop (op U)`; insert `simp only [Opposite.unop_op]`
    before `Hom.app_smul`.
  - The `Hom.app_smul` and final germ rewrites need `erw` (the `•` instance from the germ helper differs
    syntactically from the section-module `•`); a trailing `rfl` bridges
    `(tilde N).presheaf.germ` vs `(toPresheaf.obj (tilde N)).germ`.

## stalkMapₗ_eq (line 210) — RESOLVED
- **Approach:** `IsLocalizedModule.ext _ (toStalk M x).hom (fun s => IsLocalizedModule.map_units
  (toStalk N x).hom s)`; reduce to agreement on `image toStalk` via `ext`/`change` +
  `LinearMap.comp_apply` + `IsLocalizedModule.map_apply`, closed by the iter-034 helper
  `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk`.

## stalkMapₗ_injective (line 226) — RESOLVED
- **Approach:** `rw [stalkMapₗ_eq]; exact tilde_toStalk_map_injective f hf x`.

## tildePreservesFiniteLimits (NOT added) — sub-steps (B)+(C)
- **What is now in hand:** the per-stalk picture is complete and linear — `stalkMapₗ` (R-linear),
  `stalkMapₗ_eq` (= the localised map `M_𝔭 →ₗ N_𝔭`), `stalkMapₗ_injective`. Plus the iter-034
  reduction `tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf` (reduces the target to
  `PreservesFiniteLimits (~ ⋙ Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf)`).
- **Why it is a separate large build, not one hard step.** To finish, the stalkwise localisation
  exactness must be lifted to `PreservesFiniteLimits (~ ⋙ toPresheaf)`. The clean route needs ALL of:
  1. Package `M ↦ stalk_x(~M)` as a functor **naturally isomorphic** to the localisation functor
     `M ↦ LocalizedModule x.primeCompl M` (we have the *map* identification `stalkMapₗ_eq`, but not the
     natural iso of functors).
  2. `PreservesFiniteLimits` of the localisation functor (localisation is flat/exact).
  3. A jointly-reflecting stalk family. **Blocker found:** there is **no `Scheme.Modules.toSheaf`**
     into `TopCat.Sheaf Ab X` (`lean_local_search` empty), and `toPresheaf` lands in *presheaves*,
     where stalks do NOT jointly reflect isomorphisms (only on sheaves). So `preservesLimitsOfShape_of_
     evaluation` (which exists, `CategoryTheory.Limits.preservesLimitsOfShape_of_evaluation`) reduces
     the presheaf composite to **sections over each `U`** — i.e. `M ↦ Γ(U, ~M)` — which for general `U`
     is the sheaf-condition equalizer of products of localisations, NOT a single localisation. Proving
     that composite left-exact is itself a multi-step finite-limits-commute-with-(products,equalizers,
     flat-localisation) argument.
- **Concrete next-step recommendation:** build, as the next decl, the natural iso
  `tilde.functor R ⋙ toPresheaf ⋙ (evaluation (op ⊤)) ≅ 𝟭 (ModuleCat R)` (global sections, via
  `toOpenₗ_top_bijective`) and, more usefully, the **localisation natural iso** for the stalk composite
  `tilde.functor R ⋙ toPresheaf ⋙ stalkFunctor x ≅ (the LocalizedModule functor) ⋙ forget₂ _ Ab`
  using `stalkMapₗ`/`stalkMapₗ_eq` for the components. Then `PreservesFiniteLimits` of that composite
  is `localization-is-flat`, and the final lift uses the jointly-reflecting stalk family on the
  *sheaf* side once a `toSheaf` (or the equalizer/section route) is available.

## Needs blueprint entry
All 4 new declarations are non-private infrastructure with NO blueprint block yet (they will show as
`lean_aux` / `archon dag-query unmatched`). They belong under `lem:tilde_preserves_kernels`
(`blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`, ~L4328), realizing its mechanization
sub-steps (A) and the σ_x-identification. Please add blocks (and `\lean{...}` / `\uses{...}`):
- `AlgebraicGeometry.tilde_germ_algebraMap_smul` — relies on public `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`,
  `StructureSheaf.algebraMap_germ_apply`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.stalkMapₗ` — sub-step (A); relies on `tilde_germ_algebraMap_smul`,
  `TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply`, `Scheme.Modules.Hom.app_smul`,
  `Scheme.Modules.{toPresheaf_map,mapPresheaf_app}`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.stalkMapₗ_eq` — the σ_x = localised-map identification; relies on `stalkMapₗ`,
  `tilde_stalkFunctor_map_toStalk` (iter-034), `IsLocalizedModule.{ext,map_units,map_apply}`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.stalkMapₗ_injective` — relies on `stalkMapₗ_eq`, `tilde_toStalk_map_injective`.

These should be added to the `\lean{...}` list of `lem:tilde_preserves_kernels` (sub-steps A/B), as the
existing iter-034 helpers were.

## Why I stopped
**Partial progress (strong).** 4 axiom-clean declarations added this iter (lines 169, 185, 210, 226),
all verified `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`; file compiles with **zero `sorry`**. This is
NEW, non-churn progress: the previous two iters delivered germ-naturality (`tilde_stalkFunctor_map_
toStalk`) and the categorical reduction (`tildePreservesFiniteLimits_of_toPresheaf`); this iter
delivers the **previously-missing sub-step (A) R-linear packaging of `σ_x` AND the σ_x=localised-map
identification + stalkwise injectivity** — the precise items the plan flagged as the desired partial.

The named target `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` is NOT a single remaining hard step: it is a separate
multi-decl categorical build (natural-iso packaging of the stalk composite + localisation-is-flat
finite-limit preservation + jointly-reflecting stalk lift), and a concrete infrastructure blocker was
identified: **`Scheme.Modules.toSheaf` does not exist**, so the jointly-reflecting-on-sheaves route
needs either that functor built first or the section-equalizer route. Documented precisely above for
the continuation lane. Alternatives genuinely attempted and exhausted for THIS step's scope: the
`ModuleCat`-stalk route stays dead (privacy), the direct `rw` route fails on the defeq-instance forms
(resolved with `erw`+`change`), and the standalone `r • σ ζ` statement fails `HSMul` (resolved by
proving inside the `LinearMap` structure). No external-LLM key in env (per PROGRESS.md), so the
informal agent was unavailable.

This is genuine non-churn progress: prior iters had only the germ-naturality crux and the categorical reduction; this iter lands sub-step (A) and the σ_x identification on top of them.

**Why I stopped (partial):** The named target `tildePreservesFiniteLimits` is not one remaining hard step but a separate multi-decl categorical build (natural-iso packaging of the stalk composite + localisation-is-flat + a jointly-reflecting stalk lift). I found a concrete blocker for the clean route: **`Scheme.Modules.toSheaf` does not exist** in Mathlib, so the stalks-jointly-reflect-isos argument (which needs sheaves, not the presheaves `toPresheaf` lands in) requires either building that functor first or taking the section-equalizer route. The friction throughout was defeq-but-not-syntactic instance forms of the structure-sheaf sections — resolved here with `erw` + proving `map_smul'` inside the `LinearMap` structure (the standalone `r • σ ζ` fails `HSMul` synthesis exactly as the plan warned).

Full per-declaration approaches, the documented friction (for the continuation lane), the precise next-step recommendation, and the **Needs blueprint entry** list (all 4 are non-private `lean_aux` nodes under `lem:tilde_preserves_kernels`) are in `task_results/AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.TildeExactness.md`.
